title: Intensely Flavored Vietnamese Shrimp Banh Cuon
description: A briny and pillowy Vietnamese dish featuring shrimp and yellow food coloring, wrapped in a delicate banh cuon wrapper, perfect for a crowd-pleasing snack or appetizer.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together fish sauce, lime juice, grated ginger, and salt to make the marinade.
Add the peeled and deveined shrimp to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Let the shrimp marinate for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
In a small bowl, mix together chopped scallions and yellow food coloring.
Lay a banh cuon wrapper on a flat surface and brush with peanut oil.
Place a few pieces of marinated shrimp in the center of the wrapper.
Spoon some of the scallion and food coloring mixture over the shrimp.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling ingredients.
Serve the banh cuon immediately, garnished with additional chopped scallions if desired.
